channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Devs Never Considered A Happy Ending for the Game
item.content:encoded:
Sandfall Interactive never planned a happy ending for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 features multiple endings, but none offer a truly happy fairy-tale conclusion for the characters. This decision was intentional.

In a recent interview with IGN, Jennifer Svedberg-Yen stated that the team will never choose a canonical ending between the two available endings. She also explained that neither character is interested in storybook happy endings, which is why the team did not consider them during development.

“We never even considered really a true, happy ending. I think there was maybe a happier one possibly. You can see also in the way that the endings are edited, that one has a slightly more uplifting tone and one has a slightly darker tone,” she said. “Both are both good and bad. And it really just depends on whose perspective, whose good or whose happily-ever-after you are prioritizing.”

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33’s Future as a Franchise

Jennifer Svedberg-Yen was also recently asked about the possibility of the smash hit of 2025 becoming a series. She chose to remain tight-lipped, but you can learn more about that here.

Expedition 33 broke records for its critical acclaim and changed the narrative about what an AA release can be. Learn more about the game’s budget at this link.

Have you played Clair Obscur: Expedition 33? What do you think about the endings of the game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake Led by RE2 & RE4 Team, It’s Claimed

It’s claimed that the Resident Evil Code Veronica remake is being directed by the same team that helmed the Resident Evil 2 and Resident Evil 4 remakes.

Xbox Corporate VP Who Led Third-Party Relations Departing Soon

Lori Wright, the corporate vice president at Microsoft and Xbox, is departing the company.

New Call of Duty Patch Address Changes To Seasons in Black Ops 7

The latest Call of Duty patches have announced changes to how ranked seasons in Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 will end.

Crimson Desert Players Think They’ve Found ‘AI Slop’ Art

Crimson Desert players appear to have found AI-generated art throughout the game.

item.title: Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake Led by RE2 & RE4 Team, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
It’s claimed that the Resident Evil Code Veronica remake is being directed by the same team that helmed the Resident Evil 2 and Resident Evil 4 remakes.

Dusk Golem recently stated on Twitter that the forthcoming remake of Resident Evil Code Veronica, originally released in 2000 on the Sega Dreamcast, is in the hands of an experienced team.

“Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake is being directed by Kazunori Kadoi & Yasuhiro Anpo, same duo who directed Resident Evil: 2 Remake & Resident Evil: 4 Remake,” he wrote.

Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake is being directed by Kazunori Kadoi & Yasuhiro Anpo, same duo who directed Resident Evil: 2 Remake & Resident Evil: 4 Remake.

Further Comments on Resident Evil Remakes

Dusk Golem would go on to respond to some tweets about how many teams are currently working on the Resident Evil series. He would say that teams are an outdated way of thinking when it comes to developing the franchise. According to him, there are fewer set teams and a more collaborative approach, depending on the job title of Capcom employees and those who work at support studios.

“Some people work on nearly every Resident Evil game, some people work on like every third RE game, some switch between RE & other games in dev. It really depends,” he wrote.

Resident Evil Requiem’s Current Success

Resident Evil Requiem is the fastest-selling game in the franchise. In its first month on the market, it became the best-selling game of the month and is one of the highest-rated games of the year so far.

Capcom is currently looking to capitalize on the success of Resident Evil 9. They’re also looking to partner with Universal Studios Japan on a unique RE experience. Learn more about that here.

Are you excited for the upcoming Resident Evil Code Veronica remake? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Xbox Corporate VP Who Led Third-Party Relations Departing Soon
item.content:encoded:
Lori Wright, the corporate vice president at Microsoft and Xbox, is departing the company. Wright announced her departure in a LinkedIn post on March 19. No definitive departure date was specified. It was simply noted that you’d be departing in the “coming weeks.”

“I wanted to say a special thanks to the many colleagues, partners, and friends who shaped my journey. I leave with overwhelming gratitude for the adventure of a lifetime. As for what comes next, I’m hoping for a lot of beautiful sunrises and sunsets, and discovering what lies in the space in between,” she wrote.

Lori Wright worked closely with business partners and led third-party relations. Her job description notes that she “leads global partnerships & business development and marketing for Xbox, including publishing and platform partnerships, content licensing, developer programs, and market expansion.”

Xbox Continues to Undergo Massive Restructurings

2026 remains a year of significant restructuring for Xbox. Phil Spencer recently retired, and coinciding with that, Sarah Bond, the former president of Xbox, announced her resignation. In their place, Asha Sharma moved from Microsoft’s AI division to become the new head of Xbox. Alongside her, Matt Booty was named the new CCO.

In the ensuing weeks, Sharma confirmed that Project Helix remains the code name for the next-generation Xbox. She’s also continuously reaffirmed the desire to commit to the core Xbox player. Read her comments on the brand’s future here.

How do you feel about yet another shake-up at Microsoft? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: New Call of Duty Patch Address Changes To Seasons in Black Ops 7
item.content:encoded:
The latest Call of Duty patches have announced changes to how ranked seasons in Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 will end.

The latest patch notes were updated on March 20, 2026, and among changes to maps, zombies, and private matches, they announced updates to how Ranked Seasons conclude.

Changes to Call of Duty Ranked Play

In the official patch notes, the Call of Duty team confirmed that Ranked Play in Season 0 ends in 8 days. After March 25 at 9:00 p.m. PT, which is midnight on March 26 on the East Coast, players’ current Skill Ratings will be frozen. The next day, Top 250 rewards will be distributed, and a new Ranked Play experience will run for the final week of the season: the Season 2 Ranked Series.

In both Ranked Series, all competitors are enrolled in Competitive Ladders. Gold to Iridescent ladders each have 50 players. Only those from Season 02 Ranks are eligible. The Top 250 players will be on a single ladder competing for rewards like Emblems, Weapon Camos, and exclusive items.

Below are the most complete rewards for the Season 2 Ranked Series in Black Ops 7:

  • Top: #1 (Per Rank)
    1st Place Emblem
  • Top 5 (Per Rank)
    Top 5 Emblem
  • Animated Weapon Camo
    Top 10 (Per Rank)
    Top 10 Emblem
  • Top 25 (Per Rank)
    Top 25 Emblem
    Weapon Camo
  • Top 50 (Per Rank)
    Ranked Series Emblem
  • Challenge Track (All Ranks)
    Ranked Series Vinyl
    Ranked Series Charm

Season 2 Ranked Series runs from March 26-31. Then, Ranked Play will be offline until Season 3 begins.

What do you think about the changes coming to Ranked Play in Call of Duty? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Crimson Desert Players Think They’ve Found ‘AI Slop’ Art
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert players appear to have found AI-generated art throughout the game.

Pearl Abyss’ Crimson Desert launched on March 19 and has since sold millions of copies, generating considerable interest across multiple platforms. However, just a day after its release, players began to notice several pieces of art within the game that raised questions about whether they were AI-generated.

A number of threads on Reddit have popped up, pointing out various artworks that are questioned as AI-generated. IGN compiled a number of them from the Crimson Desert subreddit included some extra examples that one of their writers found as well.

AI Art in Gaming Is Becoming More of a Hot Button Issue

AI is increasingly used in game development. Recently, there was a large discussion around AI changing gaming artwork. NVIDIA recently unveiled DLSS 5. Upon seeing changes to Resident Evil’s Leon Kennedy and Grace Ashcroft, DLSS 5 was compared to AI filters that are commonly seen on social media.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang would later hit back at those who had critiqued the technology, calling naysayers “completely wrong.” His comments can be read here.

In support of the new AI-driven technology, Todd Howard confirmed that Bethesda games will use DLSS 5 going forward, starting with Starfield. Read his comments here.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide any updates regarding this story. Learn more about Crimson Desert by checking out our full review.

What do you think about the artwork found in Crimson Desert? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: LYON Eliminated by JDG From LoL First Stand
item.content:encoded:
LCS Champions LYON put up a valiant fight but were perhaps their own worst enemies in the 3-1 defeat to JD Gaming (JDG), which felt like a missed opportunity.

After G2 Esports’ shock 3-0 victory over BNK FEARX, Western fans suddenly had hope again and the pressure was on LYON to give North American supporters something to be excited about too.

However, it wasn’t to be as LPL second seeds JDG came out on top and eliminated LYON from the international League of Legends tournament.

LYON vs JDG – a game of throws

The first two matches were rather spectacular and quite surprising in that both featured rapid comebacks where the team with fewer kills won.

The first game started well for LYON as their bot-lane was getting the better of their lane opponents. Chen ‘GALA’ Wei is a fantastic talent, yet LYON’s Kim ‘Berserker’ Min-cheol was outclassing him in lane.

LYON were in firm control with Kacper ‘Inspired’ Słoma dominating the map and eventually had his team on Soul point without dropping a single kill.

But they then threw that fight for Soul, with JDG getting multiple kills, Baron, and swinging the gold 6k back down to even. Inspired had been overeager on his Nocture ultimate without any follow-up and it would cost the team dearly.

Suddenly, with that Baron buff, JDG went up the other end and took LYON’s base to win the game without doing very much. LYON overcommited with Nocturne again to get a pick on Xu ‘Xiaoxu’ Xing-Zu while the other four members of JDG ended bot.

Three minutes was all it took between JDG’s first kill and the end of the game in a remarkable swing. Yet, the same thing happened again in the next game.

LYON Respond

LYON were gapping JDG in the bot lane again, with GALA getting caught by some great Nami bubbles from Isles. Combined, LYON bot lane was 2k gold up nine minutes in.

However, JDG’s Tsai ‘HongQ’ Ming-Hong was showing why teams have been prioritising Annie as he solokilled Kang ‘Saint’ Sung-in twice in the early game.

The game swung in JDG’s favour as LYON got the first mid turret but failed to kill Yu ‘JunJia’ Chun-Chia and GALA before getting collapsed on as JDG found some kills to take a small lead.

LYON just couldn’t find fights as JDG would kite back and then re-engage with Jhin and Rumble ults to dodge while Wukong, Alistar and Annie locked everyone up.

Yet, LYON were on Soul point yet again, but their players seemed to be on completely different pages as they misplayed another fight at Drake. Saint got caught out, Inspired went in on his own again, then had to flash out, it was a mess.

On three Drakes each, JDG looked to siege LYON’s base, but the LCS champions finally played a fight correctly and secured multiple shut-downs.

Suddenly, LYON rushed to the other side of the map for a siege of their own and completed the comeback to tie the series after a 41-minute thriller.

JDG Secure Their Place in The LoL First Stand Knockouts

From there, JDG took control of the series.

Thanks to brilliant Rakan play from support Zhao ‘Vampire’ Zhe-Can, JDG dominated Game 3. LYON couldn’t get into good positions to fight and were steamrolled by JDG’s relentless teamfighting.

In the fourth game, LYON looked to have steadied themselves but once again, silly mistakes and rash decisions cost them.

An attempted Shurima Shuffle by Saint burned three of JDG’s flashes, but without Zhonyas, he was a sitting duck and died.

JDG then went on the charge, and a flash forward by Berserker’s Aphelios was barely enough damage for Niship ‘Dhokla’ Doshi’s Aatrox to get some kills, but it didn’t seem worth it and reeked of desperation.

The game then came down to coin flips, of which LYON lost both.

JDG picked up their fourth Hextech Drake for Dragon Soul as Inspired failed to win the Smite 50/50. Yet, JDG couldn’t end the game just yet.

LYON held on well until the Elder Dragon spawned, but again JDG secured the objective to effectively end the series and eliminate the LCS champions.

LYON were impressive, but it appeared that the team were playing with nerves and weren’t at their best. It’s an encouraging sign for LCS fans that they can compete, but also a frustrating missed opportunity.

Do you think LYON should have won that series? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

There is at least some good news for the North American esports ecosystem, as FlyQuest announced its Project: Grassroots initiative.

MOBILE APP
